qa.overall_assessment: All acceptance criteria verified. Both blockers identified in the prior close-debate report (AC7 literal grep failure and README staleness) were resolved by /do fixes. 49/49 pytest tests pass. All inspector reports clean for diff-introduced violations.
qa.user_verbatim_complaint: OOS items from Codex final review: (A) false positives where python3/node/ruby argv tokens trigger rm/pkill danger hooks; (B) Write-sentinel integration verification; (F) shell script cleanliness fixes (permissions, trap cleanup, stale comments).
qa.prior_close_debate_context: A prior close-debate report at docs/dev/close-report-dev-20260525-053326.md identified two blockers: (1) AC7 literal grep failure — line 44 still contained 'documentation for future implementation'; (2) cleanliness inspector introduced_in_diff:true findings for README staleness. The /do fixes resolved both. This QA report confirms both fixes are live and all ACs now pass.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: _process_script_interp() strips argv word tokens after non-shell interpreter commands; live pytest run confirms 49/49 tests pass including TestScriptInterpArgStripping class.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: function definition via git diff and direct read

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle dev-20260525-053326: _SCRIPT_INTERPS frozenset (20 interpreters) and _process_script_interp() added to hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py. strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 cleanup.py rm /tmp/old') -> 'python3   ' (rm not visible). 49/49 pytest tests pass. hooks/pre_slashcommand_validate.sh line 44: 'no-op policy stub; validation logic not yet implemented' (documentation for future implementation phrase removed). hooks/lib/README.md and hooks/tests/README.md updated to list new files. chmod 755 on 2 scripts, trap cleanup on 2 test scripts confirmed.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: only flag dangerous tokens when they appear in a position that is actually executable shell context
success_criteria_results.criterion: Stripping removes rm/killall from word tokens after script-interp cmd word
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct function call: strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 cleanup.py rm /tmp/old') -> 'python3   '. rm not present as standalone token.
success_criteria_results.details: AC1 verified. rm stripped from python3 argv.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: NOT when they appear only as string literals inside Python heredocs, codex prompt bodies, argument values
success_criteria_results.criterion: Unquoted argv to python3/node/perl/ruby stripped same as quoted args
success_criteria_results.verification_method: AC3: strip_non_executable_contexts('node script.js pkill happy') -> 'node   '. pytest TestScriptInterpArgStripping::test_node_pkill_argv_stripped PASSED.
success_criteria_results.details: AC3 verified. pkill stripped from node argv.
success_criteria_results.evidence: 49/49 pytest tests pass including node and ruby test cases

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Shell interpreter regression - rm in bash -c payload still visible

success_criteria_results.details: AC4 verified. Shell interpreter path unchanged; only _SCRIPT_INTERPS path strips word tokens.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct function call confirmed rm present in output

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Compound command - rm in second segment still visible
success_criteria_results.criterion: python3 script.py && rm /tmp/x shows rm in stripped output
success_criteria_results.verification_method: strip_non_executable_contexts('python3 script.py && rm /tmp/x') -> 'python3 &&rm /tmp/x'. rm IS present.
success_criteria_results.details: AC6 verified. Second segment processed independently; rm as command word preserved.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct function call confirmed rm present in output after &&

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) ran and produced output that was effectively the prior close-debate report (which was already written at docs/dev/close-report-dev-20260525-053326.md). Codex identified the same two blockers that the prior close-debate had: (1) AC7 literal grep failure on line 44 of hooks/pre_slashcommand_validate.sh; (2) introduced_in_diff:true README staleness findings. Codex also flagged canonical artifact absence as an observation. Both codex blockers 1 and 2 have been resolved by the /do fixes applied after the prior close-debate.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex Blocker 1 (AC7): RESOLVED — grep now returns exit 1 (no match). Confirmed: 'documentation for future implementation' phrase absent. Codex Blocker 2 (README staleness): RESOLVED — hooks/lib/README.md now lists bash_context_strip.py, hooks/tests/README.md now lists test_bash_safety_context.py. Cleanliness inspector re-run confirms resolved:true for both findings. Codex Blocker 3 (canonical artifacts): This QA report (docs/dev/qa-report-dev-20260525-053326.json) is the canonical QA artifact being written now. Codex Observations 5/6/7: noted as out-of-scope observations.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: _is_script_interp('r2') returns True because re.split splits on digits, leaving 'r' which is in _SCRIPT_INTERPS. Low practical risk (r2 is an unlikely real command name). Out-of-scope for this cycle.
Subagent Write-overwrite sentinel bypass still not covered (sentinel check inside _WG_IS_SUB != '1' guard). Original AC2 scoped to main-agent only. Separate cycle if user wants subagent coverage.
Pre-existing python3 invocations without venv activation (style-inspector finding, introduced_in_diff: false). Not introduced by this diff.
Pre-existing CJK characters in prose (style-inspector finding, introduced_in_diff: false). Not in this cycle's scope.
